elements.sent-2411-173: We introduced a novel approach to 3D head stylization that improves identity preservation and stylization quality.
elements.sent-2411-174: By utilizing the PanoHead model for 360-degree image synthesis and incorporating negative log-likelihood distillation (LD), mirror and grid score gradients, and score rank weighing, our method overcomes the limitations of previous approaches that struggled with identity retention.
elements.sent-2411-175: Experimental results show significant qualitative and quantitative improvements, advancing the state of 3D head stylization.
elements.sent-2411-176: Our work also provides valuable insights into effective distillation processes between diffusion models and GANs, emphasizing the importance of identity preservation in stylization tasks.

elements.sent-2411-340: Regarding the motivation for LD, our findings align with prior research, which suggests that SDS tends to collapse into fixed patterns and over-smoothing due to its mode-seeking nature.
elements.sent-2411-341: This is a tradeoff to avoid divergence, as mentioned in the original SDS paper (DreamFusion).
elements.sent-2411-342: In contrast, LD is diversity-seeking, which is more suitable for our task, considering that we utilize a GAN prior.
elements.sent-2411-343: We believe our work can open new avenues for distillation to generative models without disrupting their well-regularized latent spaces.
elements.sent-2411-344: While we have not modified the LD itself, we incorporate rank-weighted scores and combine them with multi-view and mirror-based distillation.
elements.sent-2411-345: Additionally, our improvements include clever engineering efforts, such as bypassing the super-resolver when using the grid structure.
elements.sent-2411-346: Regarding the decrease in ID score with the grid operation, we observe that the view-consistent gradients updating the generator enable the model to achieve higher stylization, which results in ID score decrease.
elements.sent-2411-347: For instance, in the Pixar example in Fig. 8, grid operation produces more pronounced changes, while the one without the grid makes more subtle adjustments.
elements.sent-2411-348: This may lead to higher ID scores, such as the chin being closer to the input image.
